mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat at the lower bound and CPIAUCSL continues to rise, real policy rates fall and discount-rate pressure on equities eases, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS stays unchanged for 2+ months while CPIAUCSL rises, or if the sign of the conditional return effect is negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent path signals deteriorating household income and weaker demand expectations, which depresses equity prices and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances after a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise, or if the short-horizon effect is not negative in at least two consecutive rolling 2-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broad payroll growth and firm retail sales, confirming resilient final demand that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous positive monthly changes in PAYEMS and RSAFS do not outperform single-series conditions, or if triggered instances generate negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO without a matching increase in CPIAUCSL signals productivity-led real output growth rather than overheating, which should support equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average while CPIAUCSL monthly inflation remains below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO surprises combined with subdued CPIAUCSL fail to produce above-baseline 60-day returns, or if the conditional return distribution is indistinguishable from zero over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals housing demand weakness and tighter wealth effects, which leads to weaker risk appetite and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% versus its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day returns are positive in a majority of periods following an 8% HOUST decline versus its 6-month average, or if the conditional effect disappears after accounting for recession windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in FEDFUNDS after a tightening peak transmits easier financial conditions through lower discount rates and credit stress, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 50 bps from a prior 6-month peak
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX forward returns are not higher after 50 bps rate cuts from a peak, or if rate-cut episodes do not outperform unchanged-rate episodes in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises, the labor market is losing momentum and earnings expectations weaken, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if combined PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not yield below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not negative and significant in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a broad demand rebound, higher GDPC1 growth together with rising RSAFS confirms accelerating nominal activity, which should lift equities over the next quarter; the signal fails when growth is purely inventory-driven or GDP is revised away.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth turns positive and RSAFS is above its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth plus above-average RSAFS does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect vanishes after excluding periods with large GDP revisions.
